channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: ROSWELL, Ga. (AP) — A hawk swooped in and landed on a tree, then watched as Michele and Jeremy Davis mourned their murdered son at a vigil outside his high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New EPA guidance says spending items greater than $50,000 must get approval from DOGE
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency has issued new guidance directing that spending items greater than $50,000 now require approval from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A timeline of how actor Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa died at their New Mexico home
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Actor Gene Hackman died of heart disease a full week after his wife died from hantavirus in their New Mexico home, likely unaware that she was dead because he was in the advanced stages of Alzheimer’s disease, authorities revealed Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — From heartbreak and devastation to outrage, Emily Pike's tragic death is stirring heavy emotions and spotlighting a crisis that has long plagued Native American communities, where a disproportionate number of people have been killed or have gone missing.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawsuit aims to strike down LGBTQ antidiscrimination protections in Pennsylvania
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Two public school districts and several parents have sued Pennsylvania in a bid to undo antidiscrimination protections for gay and transgender people, saying the state's two-year-old regulation is illegal because it goes beyond what lawmakers intended or allowed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moon mission goes sideways: Private lunar lander declared dead after botched landing
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon’s south pole and its mission is over, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: By MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, Fox 5

item.title: States sue President Trump’s administration over mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Maryland and 19 other states are suing multiple federal agencies, contending President Donald Trump's administration has illegally fired thousands of federal probationary workers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Targeted Tesla attacks continue amid outrage at Elon Musk
item.description: Some Tesla showrooms, dealerships, and charging stations have been set on fire or vandalized in other ways, ostensibly in protest of Musk's role.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: CEO of Jack Daniel's responds after whiskey pulled from Canada's shelves
item.description: The Distilled Spirits Council of the United States applauded President Donald Trump’s decision to suspend tariffs one day after some Canadian provinces started pulling U.S. liquor, including Jack Daniel's, from shelves.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, KRON4

item.title: Flight attendant pleads guilty to secretly recording a 14-year-old girl in plane bathroom
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A former American Airlines flight attendant has pleaded guilty to secretly recording video of a 14-year-old girl using an airplane bathroom and having recordings of four other girls using the lavatories.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump NATO stance shows 'simple-minded view of the world': Bolton
item.description: Trump said the U.S. may not help defend NATO members that fail to meet their military spending commitment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: A court-appointed lawyer has advised a federal judge to let the Justice Department drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams but prevent prosecutors from ever reviving the case so it doesn't hang over him “like the proverbial Sword of Damocles.”.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Pennsylvania State Police stated that on February 22, troopers began investigating the theft of music equipment from a storage unit at D.M.D Mini Storage in Penn Township, Snyder County.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, KRON4

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy Amphibious Base Coronado in San Diego County has been the subject of controversy for decades due to the exterior design of four barracks that resemble a swastika.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: New Mexico man found guilty of 'decapitating' man, 'playing soccer' with his head
item.description: Arciniega-Saenz, a homeless person at the time of the crime, stabbed Garcia 84 times with a small switchblade, the DA's Office said.
item.dc:creator: Luisa Barrios, NewsNation

item.title: Trump raises doubts about NATO's mutual defense pact
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested the United States may not defend NATO allies that have not contributed enough to defense spending, casting doubt on a core aspect of the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Former Olympian turned drug kingpin named to FBI's 10 Most Wanted list
item.description: A former Olympian has been added to the FBI's 10 Most Wanted Fugitives list for allegedly leading a global drug operation that shipped hundreds of kilograms of cocaine across North America and ordering multiple murders.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: President Trump delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump Thursday paused the 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico that started earlier this week, with plans to restart them in April.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KTLA 5

item.title: No prison time for man who bashed, disfigured mother’s face in front of child
item.description: Trevor Lee Colombano, 38, faced up to nine years in state prison in addition to 18 months in county jail after pleading no contest to assault with a deadly weapon, sexual battery and mayhem.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon man accused of throwing Molotov cocktails, shooting at Tesla dealership
item.description: Adam Matthew Lansky, 41, has been arraigned on federal charges after being accused of attempting to destroy property at a Tesla dealership twice.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, KRON4

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Three Michigan brothers missing since 2010 are declared dead by a judge at mom's request
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved despite an explicit belief by investigators that their father is responsible.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan brothers missing since 2010 declared dead at mom's request
item.description: A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
